items.title: Billionaires Couldn't Get Enough of This ETF in 2024. Is It Right for You in 2025?
items.author: Jeremy Bowman
items.description: The article discusses the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) and its popularity among billionaire investors, including Warren Buffett. It highlights the ETF's low cost, diversification, and strong historical performance as reasons for its appeal.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF's low cost, diversification, and strong historical performance as reasons for its appeal among billionaire investors and the general public.
The article mentions that Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway owns shares of the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF, indicating a positive sentiment towards the investment.
The article mentions that Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway owns shares of the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF, indicating a positive sentiment towards the investment.
The article states that some billionaire investors own shares of other S&P 500 ETFs, such as the SPDR S&P 500 ETF, suggesting a positive sentiment towards S&P 500 index funds in general.
The article mentions that some billionaire investors own shares of the iShares Core S&P 500 ETF, indicating a positive sentiment towards S&P 500 index funds.
